frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Honestly, I always feel a different level of respect for women once I know they’re someone’s wife or mother. A woman carries an entire family on her shoulders, and that role itself deserves honour beyond anything else.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames women’s worth and respect primarily through relational roles like wife and mother rather than as individuals. It encodes conditional respect and personhood erasure while sounding superficially respectful.
original_frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 29. Married two years ago. A woman working at an MNC in Kolkata. You are Bengali and your tradition is shankha pola Ñ the white and red bangles married Bengali women wear. You wore them for the first year of your marriage but have recently stopped wearing them to office because they make noise during meetings and your wrists get irritated. Your mother just found out from your aunt who saw you at a family function without them. Your mother has called you. You are in the middle of that phone call right now. You are trying to explain your practical reasons. Your mother is not interested in your practical reasons.
